entities.name: COMT
entities.context: Gene variant related to dopamine metabolism; linked to focus collapse mid-day

entities.name: FTO
entities.context: Gene variant related to hunger signaling; causes persistent hunger despite eating

entities.name: Vyvanse
entities.context: Stimulant medication V takes; timing affects appetite, focus, and sleep

entities.name: Dopamine Depletion
entities.context: Neurochemical cause of mid-day focus collapse, linked to COMT variant

entities.name: Stimulant Appetite Suppression
entities.context: Side effect of Vyvanse causing no appetite until evening

entities.name: Caffeine Cutoff Protocol
entities.context: Strict 2pm caffeine cutoff to prevent afternoon brain fog and racing thoughts at night

entities.name: Decision Fatigue
entities.context: Result of sustained cognitive load; mitigated by externalizing decisions and using frameworks

entities.name: Sleep Debt
entities.context: Contributor to irritability spikes, compounded by stress

entities.name: Front-Loading Breakfast
entities.context: Mitigation strategy for stimulant appetite suppression; eat before medication suppresses hunger

entities.name: Warning Signals Framework
entities.context: V's systematic framework mapping physiological signals to likely causes and mitigations

relationships.context: COMT gene variant contributes to dopamine depletion causing focus collapse
Both affect V's hunger/appetite patterns through different mechanisms
Vyvanse causes appetite suppression requiring front-loaded meals
Late Vyvanse combined with late caffeine causes racing thoughts at night
Stimulant wearing off contributes to dopamine depletion and focus collapse
Front-loading breakfast is the primary mitigation for stimulant appetite suppression
Decision fatigue is one of the warning signals V tracks; mitigated by externalizing decisions
Caffeine cutoff protocol helps prevent sleep debt accumulation
